title: Business Template
text_content:
If you want business to be plug-and-play, you have to stop thinking in terms of companies and start thinking in terms of modules, interfaces, and constraints.
What you’re really asking for is a Business Operating Template (BOT) — a repeatable system where ideas, people, and capital can be swapped without breaking the machine.
Below is the cleanest way to do this at a systems-engineering level, not hustle fluff.

1. The Prime Directive (Non-Negotiable)
Every business must be decomposed into replaceable modules with defined inputs, outputs, and governance rules.
If a component cannot be replaced without collapsing the business, it is not a system — it’s a personality-dependent operation.

2. The Universal Business Skeleton (The Chassis)
Every plug-and-play business runs on the same 6 core layers:
1. Market Interface
2. Value Engine
3. Delivery Engine
4. Growth Engine
5. Operations Engine
6. Capital & Governance Engine
Think of this like a motherboard. Every venture snaps into the same slots.

3. Layer 1: Market Interface (Demand Adapter)
Purpose: Capture attention → qualify demand → convert.
Never custom-build this per business. Ever.
Standard Inputs
ICP definition (1 page max)
Primary pain → desired outcome
Proof asset (case study, demo, authority)
Standard Outputs
Qualified lead
Offer selection
Data feedback loop
Plug-and-Play Components
Content → DM → Call funnel
VSL → Application → Close
Outbound → Booking → Close
Rule: The interface stays the same; only the message swaps.

4. Layer 2: Value Engine (Offer Core)
Purpose: Transform input (customer state) into output (result).
Standardized Offer Architecture
Every offer must be expressible as:
INPUT STATE → PROCESS → OUTPUT STATE
Example:
Input: “Founder with traffic, no conversion”
Process: “Positioning + funnel + scripts”
Output: “Predictable booked calls”
Offer Constraints
One primary promise
One measurable outcome
One delivery method
If it needs explanation longer than 60 seconds, it’s not a module.

5. Layer 3: Delivery Engine (Execution Factory)
This is where most founders break the system.
Rule
The founder never delivers by default. They only design delivery.
Delivery Templates
Async course
SOP-driven service
Coaching w/ scripts
AI-assisted execution
Required Artifacts
SOPs (step-by-step, no judgment calls)
QA checklists
Escalation rules
Time caps per unit
If delivery quality depends on “talent,” it’s not templatized yet.

6. Layer 4: Growth Engine (Traffic & Expansion)
Growth must be independent from delivery.
Standard Growth Loops
Content flywheel
Referral loop
Paid traffic loop
Partnerships
Each loop has:
Trigger
Action
Reward
Metric
You are not scaling a business — you’re scaling loops.

7. Layer 5: Operations Engine (Replace the Human)
This is the anti-fragility layer.
Every role is defined as:
INPUT → DECISION RULES → OUTPUT
No role may rely on:
Memory
Motivation
“Good judgment”
Core Ops Templates
Sales ops
Fulfillment ops
Support ops
Finance ops
If a human leaves and knowledge leaves with them, the system failed.

8. Layer 6: Capital & Governance Engine (The Firewall)
This prevents chaos as you add businesses.
Standard Rules
Separate entity per venture
Central IP holding company
Shared services layer (marketing, ops, AI, finance)
Monthly kill / scale / fix decisions
Capital Flow
Venture → Holding Co → Allocation Engine → Reinvestment
No emotional reinvestment. Only rules.

9. The Plug-In Test (Critical)
A business is plug-and-play only if:
✅ You can swap the offer
✅ You can swap the operator
✅ You can swap the traffic source
✅ You can pause growth without collapse
✅ You can sell the unit without explaining it
If any answer is “no,” it’s still artisanal.

10. What This Enables (Why This Matters)
Once templatized, you can:
Launch new ventures in weeks, not months
Acquire businesses and snap them into your chassis
Delegate execution to humans or AI
Build a portfolio, not a job
Turn identity into IP, not dependency
You stop building businesses.
You start deploying systems.

Next Step (If You Want)
If you want, I can:
Turn this into a one-page master template
Design a Notion / Obsidian schema
Create a Business Plug-In Spec (like an API doc)
Map this directly onto your current ecosystem
Tell me which artifact you want first.
